description_long: This is a classic upright arcade game cabinet with a woodgrain finish, reminiscent of early video game machines from the 1970s. The front features a slanted control panel with two rotary knobs labeled for Player 1 and Player 2, and a coin slot below. Above the control panel is a large, vertically oriented display area with a yellow bezel and a simple black-and-white game screen, marked with the word 'PONG' at the top. The cabinet's sides are plain, with a distinctive angular cutout near the front, revealing a glimpse of the yellow bezel from the side. The top and back are flat and unadorned, emphasizing the utilitarian design. The overall construction appears sturdy, with the main structure likely made of wood or particleboard, and the control panel and screen area framed in metal and plastic.
description: A vintage upright arcade game cabinet with a woodgrain finish, yellow bezel, and a slanted control panel featuring two rotary knobs and a coin slot. The front display area is marked 'PONG' and the sides are plain with an angular cutout.
description_view_0: Side view showing the woodgrain finish and a distinctive angular cutout near the front, partially revealing the yellow bezel.
description_view_1: Rear view displaying a flat, unadorned woodgrain panel with no visible features or openings.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the first side, with the same angular cutout and plain woodgrain surface.
description_view_3: Front view highlighting the yellow bezel, 'PONG' display, slanted control panel with two rotary knobs, and a coin slot below.
attribute_screen_type: CRT display, vertically oriented, flat with a visible bezel and simple black-and-white graphics.
attribute_control_panel_layout: Two rotary knobs side-by-side for two players, labeled Player 1 and Player 2, with a white panel and black text.
attribute_marquee_artwork: The marquee area is plain with the word 'PONG' in black text on a yellow background; no elaborate artwork.
